title: Substack adds an optional Pangram scan for AI-written text
friendlyTitle: Substack can estimate whether a post used AI writing
summary: Substack readers can now scan eligible posts and notes with Pangram to estimate what percentage of the text is human-written or AI-assisted. The feature applies to content published on or after 21 July 2026 and presents a probability-based estimate rather than proof of authorship.
friendlySummary: Readers can ask Substack to scan newer posts and notes and estimate how much of the text may be human or AI-written.
whyItMatters: The feature gives readers another transparency signal as AI-assisted publishing grows, but detection scores can be misunderstood or wrong. Writers and readers should treat the result as a prompt for disclosure and judgement, not as a verdict on quality or honesty.
friendlyWhyItMatters: The score may be helpful, but it is only an estimate and cannot prove who wrote something or whether it is good.
